metadata.title: Home | Alessio Quagliara
metadata.description: Indie hacker and AI engineering student building small SaaS products, automation experiments, and operational tools in public.

home.badge: Indie Hacker · Building SaaS in Public · AI Engineering Student
home.title: Indie Hacker Building SaaS in Public | AI Engineering Student
home.description: Computer Engineering student interested in software, industrial automation, and AI. I publicly document the process of building small SaaS products, software experiments, and operational tools inspired by real warehouse and production problems.

whatIBuild.title: What I Build
whatIBuild.subtitle: I build and document small, concrete software experiments connected to real operational problems.

cards.title: SaaS built in public
cards.text: I am building small SaaS products and documenting the process: technical choices, mistakes, iterations, and lessons learned without pretending to be more senior than I am.

cards.title: Operational tools
cards.text: I care about simple tools for warehouses, production, QR workflows, tracking, and daily operations where software can remove manual friction.

cards.title: Physical automation
cards.text: I keep Arduino, ESP32, and basic embedded systems close because they connect code, sensors, modules, and real problems outside the screen.

howIWork.title: How I Work
howIWork.subtitle: I am learning seriously by building in public: fewer claims, more practice, more clarity about what I can really do.
howIWork.quote: I do not use complexity to impress. I remove it until the project breathes better.

cards.title: Build before claiming
cards.text: I prefer showing real progress, prototypes, and development notes instead of presenting myself as an expert in things I am still consolidating.

cards.title: Simplicity before stack
cards.text: I choose tools I understand and can maintain. If Flask, Quart, or HTML/CSS are enough, I start there and add complexity only when it is needed.

cards.title: Real problems
cards.text: I work best where software, automation, logistics, and physical systems meet: warehouse, production, monitoring, and small daily inefficiencies.

selectedProjects.title: Selected Projects
selectedProjects.subtitle: A selection of projects showing what I am building and learning across product, automation, and real systems.
selectedProjects.intro: I am not interested in filling a portfolio with disconnected experiments. I would rather show a few projects that genuinely represent how I build software.
selectedProjects.eyebrow: Each project points in a clear direction: small SaaS, automation, AI/LLM tooling, or operational software.

technicalFocus.title: Technical Focus
technicalFocus.subtitle: No stack flexing: these are the areas I am using, studying, and consolidating through actual projects.

blocks.title: Backend & Web
blocks.text: I am building backend applications with Flask and Quart, plus simple and maintainable HTML/CSS interfaces

blocks.title: Core Skills
blocks.text: C, systems fundamentals, data structures, application logic, and disciplined code reading

blocks.title: AI & Tooling
blocks.text: AI/LLM tooling, API automations, small agents, assisted workflows, and AI engineering study

blocks.title: Hardware & Automation
blocks.text: Arduino, ESP32, Raspberry Pi, sensors, QR workflows, basic embedded prototypes, and physical operations

contactCta.title: Let's talk
contactCta.text: If you want to follow the process, discuss a real operational problem, or exchange ideas about automation, AI, and physical systems, send me a message.

metadata.title: About | Alessio Quagliara
metadata.description: Alessio Quagliara's background and technical focus: Computer Engineering student, indie hacker, and public builder of small SaaS products, automation, and operational tools.

about.title: About
about.intro: I am a Computer Engineering student interested in software, industrial automation, and AI. I am rebuilding my positioning around something more true: learning seriously by building in public.

sections.title: My approach
sections.text: I do not want to communicate more seniority than I actually feel. I prefer showing what I am building, what I am learning, and how I choose pragmatic solutions.

sections.title: What I want to build
sections.text: I care about small SaaS products, automations, warehouse and production tools, QR workflows, operational dashboards, and tools born from concrete problems.

sections.title: Software and the real world
sections.text: Arduino, ESP32, and automation differentiate me from a typical web-only developer: I like when software touches sensors, modules, physical objects, and operational processes.

about.closing: My goal is to become stronger by building real things, documenting the path, and connecting software, AI, automation, and concrete operational problems.

cta.title: Want to talk?
cta.text: If you want to follow the path or discuss a concrete problem across software, automation, and physical systems, we can talk.

metadata.title: Contact | Alessio Quagliara
metadata.description: Contact to discuss small SaaS products, automation, AI/LLM tooling, warehouse, production, and operational software.

contact.title: Let's talk
contact.intro: If you want to follow the journey, exchange ideas, or tell me about a real operational problem, send me a message.
contact.description: I am mainly interested in small SaaS products, industrial automation, QR workflows, warehouse and production tools, AI/LLM tooling, and bridges between software and the physical world.
bullets: You want to follow the public build of small SaaS products
You have a manual warehouse or production workflow to clarify
You want to exchange ideas about Flask, Quart, AI tooling, or automation
You care about connecting software, Arduino, ESP32, and physical systems
contact.secondaryCta: Prefer writing first? Send me an email.

metadata.title: Education | Alessio Quagliara
metadata.description: Continuous education path across automation, software development, business perspective, and a Computer Engineering degree focused on intelligent systems.

education.title: An education built across engineering, software, and real systems
education.intro: My education comes from the intersection of engineering, software development, operational processes, and business perspective. Today it continues with an even stronger focus on AI, intelligent systems, automation, and real solution design.
education.learningStatement: For me, continuous learning is not filler work: it is how I connect technical growth, design capability, and long-term vision. For {years} years I have invested in study with this mindset.

items.title: Bachelor's Degree in Computer Engineering - Intelligent Systems
items.description: University path focused on advanced programming, artificial intelligence, machine learning, neural networks, embedded systems, IoT, cybersecurity, and databases, with attention to intelligent systems and software design.

items.title: Diploma in Administration, Finance and Marketing
items.description: A path that strengthened my understanding of accounting, marketing, business logic, and project sustainability, adding a perspective that is useful when building software products and services.

items.title: Full Stack Web Developer Certification
items.description: An intensive path focused on end-to-end web application design and development, which consolidated practical foundations across frontend, backend, databases, and product logic.

items.title: Diploma in Automation Technology
items.description: Technical training that built the foundations of my relationship with industrial automation, electronics, networks, and programming, and still shapes how I think about systems, hardware, and real-world integration.

cookie.title: Your privacy matters
cookie.subtitle: This site uses only strictly necessary cookies and, if enabled, anonymous measurement tools.
cookie.description: Necessary cookies help the site function properly. Any additional cookies, if present, are used only to understand in aggregate how the site is used and improve it over time.
cookie.whatAreCookiesText: Cookies are small text files saved on your device while browsing. They can serve site functionality, save preferences, or in some cases, measure service usage.
